description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, roughly hewn gray stone blocks, forming a robust and ancient-looking structure. The archway is supported by two thick pillars, each adorned with leafy green foliage and small orange flowers, suggesting a blend of natural and architectural elements. The top of the arch is composed of several stacked stone slabs, giving it a rugged, weathered appearance. The interior of the arch is open, allowing passage through the structure, and the underside is shadowed, emphasizing its depth. Decorative stonework and plant motifs are visible at the base and midsection of the pillars, adding visual interest. The overall design evokes a sense of antiquity, reminiscent of ruins found in overgrown urban parks or historical sites.
description: A rugged stone triumphal arch with leafy green and orange floral decorations on its pillars, evoking an ancient, overgrown monument.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway, both decorated pillars, and the leafy and floral details.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the stone structure and the depth of the archway.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open passage and the stacked stone construction from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the stone block layering and partial visibility of the decorative foliage.
